introduction: Tuberculosis (TB) is one of the oldest diseases known to affect humans. It is caused by bacteria of the mycotuberculosis complex which includes M. tuberculosis, M. bovis, and M. africanum. M. tuberculosis is the most common cause of tuberculosis worldwide. Transmission is by droplet infection. Nigeria ranks 11th among the high burden countries of TB in the world. The Federal government of Nigeria established the National Tuberculosis and Leprosy Control Program (NTBLCS) in 1993 with the objective to reduce the prevalence of TB and Leprosy to a level at which they no longer constitute a public health problem in the country. Almost every organ can be affected. The lung parenchyma are affected in more than 80% of the cases. This can be either as primary pulmonary disease (occurs mainly in childhood) or post-primary pulmonary disease. Those at risk of acquiring tuberculosis include contacts of patients with smear-positive pulmonary disease, immunocompromised individuals, health workers, and people living in overcrowded conditions.

features: Crepitations usually in the upper zone (earliest physical sign)
Physical signs of consolidation, cavitation and fibrosis develop later
Erythema nodosum and phlyctenular conjunctivitis (Primary PTB)

investigations: Bacteriologic examination: Sputum AFB X 3 (spot, morning, spot)
Sputum mycobacterial culture and drug susceptibility testing/Gene Xpert MTB-RIF Assay
Tuberculin skin test (low sensitivity and low specificity)
Fibre-optic bronchoscopy with bronchoalveolar lavage and trans-bronchial biopsy

goals: Prevent death from active TB or its late effects
Prevent the development of acquired drug resistance
drug: Initial Intensive Phase: 2 months using Rifampicin, isoniazid, pyrazinamide and ethambutol
Continuation Phase: 4 months using Isoniazid and rifampicin
Pyridoxine 10mg is added to prevent peripheral neuropathy
Fixed dose combinations or loose tablets
adverse_reactions_and_cautions: Rifampicin: Drug interactions, hypersensitivity, hepatitis, fever
Potential interactions with rifampicin and antiretroviral agents
Rifabutin may be used in place of rifampicin but it's not easily available and expensive
Increased incidence of drug resistance cases
supportive_measures: Care and management in DOTs centres

differential_diagnoses: Lung abscess due to Klebsiella pneumoniae
